_instructions: Add ArXiv papers to 'queue' to feature them in the next research pipeline run. They are processed one at a time (oldest first) and moved to 'archive' automatically. Accepts full ArXiv URLs (https://arxiv.org/abs/XXXX) or bare IDs (XXXX.XXXXX). Auto-rotation: when queue is empty, the pipeline randomly (~33% of runs) injects a landmark paper from 'archive' as a Classic Paper Series post. recently_featured tracks last 12 featured URLs to avoid repetition.

archive.note: Attention Is All You Need — the Transformer architecture that started everything (Vaswani et al., Google, 2017)
BERT — bidirectional pre-training that revolutionised NLP (Devlin et al., Google, 2018)
GPT-3 — Language Models are Few-Shot Learners, scaling laws in action (Brown et al., OpenAI, 2020)
InstructGPT — Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback / RLHF (Ouyang et al., OpenAI, 2022)
LLaMA 2 — Open foundation models from Meta, democratised LLM access (Touvron et al., Meta, 2023)
LLaMA 3 — Meta's next-generation open foundation models, state-of-the-art open weights (Dubey et al., Meta, 2024)
Mixtral of Experts — efficient sparse mixture-of-experts LLM from Mistral AI (Jiang et al., Mistral, 2024)
DeepSeek-R1 — reasoning via reinforcement learning without supervised fine-tuning, shook the industry Jan 2025
Vision Transformer (ViT) — An Image is Worth 16x16 Words, transformers conquer computer vision (Dosovitskiy et al., Google, 2020)
Latent Diffusion Models (Stable Diffusion) — high-resolution image generation in latent space (Rombach et al., LMU Munich, 2022)
LLaVA — Visual Instruction Tuning, multimodal LLMs with vision (Liu et al., UW Madison, 2023)
Chain-of-Thought Prompting — step-by-step reasoning dramatically improves LLM performance (Wei et al., Google Brain, 2022)
RAG — Retrieval-Augmented Generation, grounding LLMs in external knowledge (Lewis et al., Facebook AI, 2020)
ReAct — Reasoning + Acting in LLMs, the foundation for AI agents (Yao et al., Princeton/Google, 2022)
HuggingGPT — LLMs as controllers for multi-tool AI systems, early agentic AI (Shen et al., Microsoft/ZJU, 2023)
AutoGen — multi-agent conversation framework from Microsoft Research (Wu et al., Microsoft, 2023)
AlphaFold Multimer — protein complex structure prediction, Nobel Prize-winning work (Evans et al., DeepMind, 2022)
Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models — Kaplan et al., why bigger models trained on more data work better (Kaplan et al., OpenAI, 2020)
Chinchilla — Training Compute-Optimal LLMs, changed how the field thinks about data vs parameters (Hoffmann et al., DeepMind, 2022)
Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) — simpler, stabler alternative to RLHF for alignment (Rafailov et al., Stanford, 2023)
LoRA — Low-Rank Adaptation, parameter-efficient fine-tuning that the entire field now uses (Hu et al., Microsoft, 2021)
QLoRA — quantised LoRA, fine-tune 65B models on a single consumer GPU (Dettmers et al., UW, 2023)
Flash Attention — IO-aware exact attention that made long-context models practical (Dao et al., Stanford, 2022)
Mamba — selective state space models, the leading structured alternative to transformers (Gu & Dao, CMU/Princeton, 2023)
KAN — Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks, a fundamentally different neural network architecture beyond MLPs (Liu et al., MIT, 2024)
VAE — Variational Autoencoders, foundational generative model with latent variable inference (Kingma & Welling, 2013)
GANs — Generative Adversarial Networks, Ian Goodfellow's landmark paper that defined a generation of image synthesis (Goodfellow et al., 2014)
DDPM — Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models, the paper behind the modern diffusion revolution (Ho et al., UC Berkeley, 2020)
GPT-4 Technical Report — multimodal capabilities and evals at scale (OpenAI, 2023)
Gemini — Google DeepMind's multimodal model technical report and benchmark results (Team Gemini, 2023)
Gemini 1.5 — 1M token context window via Mixture of Experts, breakthrough long-context reasoning (Team Gemini, 2024)
Sora — OpenAI video generation, world simulators as video models trained on internet-scale data (Brooks et al., OpenAI, 2024)
DQN — Playing Atari with Deep Reinforcement Learning, DeepMind's breakthrough combining RL with deep networks (Mnih et al., DeepMind, 2013)
PPO — Proximal Policy Optimization, the RL algorithm powering RLHF training (Schulman et al., OpenAI, 2017)
AlphaGo Zero — mastering Go from self-play with no human knowledge, self-supervised RL (Silver et al., DeepMind, 2017)
Constitutional AI — Anthropic's approach to AI safety and alignment via self-critique (Bai et al., Anthropic, 2022)
Mistral 7B — punching above its weight, grouped-query attention and sliding window for efficient open-source LLM (Jiang et al., Mistral AI, 2023)
Phi-3 — Microsoft's small language models that outperform much larger models via high-quality data curation (Abdin et al., Microsoft, 2024)
CLIP — Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training, connecting vision and language at scale (Radford et al., OpenAI, 2021)
DALL-E 2 — hierarchical text-to-image generation with CLIP priors and diffusion (Ramesh et al., OpenAI, 2022)
Multiple Physics Pretraining (MPP) — the 'cat videos vs physics' paper from PolymathicAI / Cranmer et al., 2023
Radio Galaxy Zoo — Anna Scaife's self-supervised foundation model for radio astronomy (Scaife et al., Manchester, 2023)
ColBERT — Contextualized Late Interaction over BERT, efficient passage retrieval for dense neural search (Khattab & Zaharia, Stanford, 2020)
